title: Human Resources Specialists
description: Recruit, screen, interview, or place individuals within an organization. May perform other activities in multiple human resources areas.

profiles.description: Interpret and explain human resources policies, procedures, laws, standards, or regulations.
profiles.rationale: Because 'interpret and explain ... policies, procedures, laws, standards, or regulations' entails compliance interpretation with liability, a human must handle it; SHRM compliance workload guidance suggests roughly ~1 hour weekly on policy interpretation and employee guidance.

profiles.description: Hire employees and process hiring-related paperwork.
profiles.rationale: AI can prefill forms and checklists for 'process hiring-related paperwork' while a human makes final hiring decisions and validations; SHRM onboarding benchmarks place hiring paperwork at ~45 minutes per hire.

profiles.description: Administer employee benefit plans.
profiles.rationale: Administering 'employee benefit plans' is rule-heavy and AI can draft enrollments/answers but humans finalize and resolve exceptions; SHRM benefits administration benchmarks indicate ~1.5 hours weekly per HR specialist.

profiles.description: Maintain current knowledge of Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and affirmative action guidelines and laws, such as the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA).
profiles.rationale: Maintaining knowledge of 'EEO ... ADA' can be summarized by AI but requires human judgment for compliance, so assistance is appropriate; SHRM legal/compliance update practices indicate ~45 minutes weekly spent on regulatory reading.

profiles.description: Schedule or administer skill, intelligence, psychological, or drug tests for current or prospective employees.
profiles.rationale: Scheduling or administering 'skill, intelligence, psychological, or drug tests' is standardized workflow that tools can run end-to-end; ATS vendor data (Lever/Greenhouse) show ~5–15 minutes manually per candidate which automation reduces to a brief spot-check.

profiles.description: Prepare or maintain employment records related to events, such as hiring, termination, leaves, transfers, or promotions, using human resources management system software.
profiles.rationale: 'Prepare or maintain employment records ... using human resources management system software' is structured record-keeping that HRIS can auto-populate; HRIS/ATS benchmarks show ~10–20 minutes manually per event, near-zero with automation aside from review.

profiles.description: Address employee relations issues, such as harassment allegations, work complaints, or other employee concerns.
profiles.rationale: Addressing 'harassment allegations, work complaints, or other employee concerns' requires sensitive, high-liability human conversation, so AI cannot replace the human; SHRM employee relations case-handling guidance suggests ~60 minutes per meeting.

profiles.description: Conduct reference or background checks on job applicants.
profiles.rationale: For 'reference or background checks' AI can initiate vendor orders and summarize notes, but humans must handle consent and evaluate discrepancies; SHRM recruiting benchmarks indicate ~20–30 minutes per candidate.

profiles.description: Inform job applicants of details such as duties and responsibilities, compensation, benefits, schedules, working conditions, or promotion opportunities.
profiles.rationale: Informing applicants of 'duties ... compensation, benefits, schedules' can be drafted by AI but should be delivered/confirmed by a human due to pay and legal nuance; LinkedIn Talent Solutions workflow surveys show ~10 minutes per candidate communication.

profiles.description: Review employment applications and job orders to match applicants with job requirements.
profiles.rationale: Reviewing 'employment applications and job orders to match applicants with job requirements' is standardized screening well-suited to AI parsing and matching; SHRM/ATS benchmarks cite ~5 minutes per application manually, reduced to a quick check with automation.

profiles.description: Select qualified job applicants or refer them to managers, making hiring recommendations when appropriate.
profiles.rationale: 'Select qualified job applicants or refer them to managers, making hiring recommendations' benefits from AI ranking/shortlists but requires human discretion and accountability; LinkedIn Talent Solutions reports ~10–20 minutes per candidate decision.

profiles.description: Schedule or conduct new employee orientations.
profiles.rationale: 'Schedule or conduct new employee orientations' can be scheduled and content prepped by AI, but delivery and Q&A are human-led; SHRM onboarding practices estimate ~60 minutes per new-hire session.

profiles.description: Review and evaluate applicant qualifications or eligibility for specified licensing, according to established guidelines and designated licensing codes.
profiles.rationale: Reviewing 'applicant qualifications or eligibility for specified licensing ... codes' can be rule-checked by AI but requires human sign-off; SHRM compliance staffing guidance indicates ~15–25 minutes per candidate.

profiles.description: Maintain and update human resources documents, such as organizational charts, employee handbooks or directories, or performance evaluation forms.
profiles.rationale: Maintaining 'organizational charts, employee handbooks or directories, or performance evaluation forms' is document upkeep AI can draft/format but humans approve; SHRM HR administration benchmarks suggest ~30–60 minutes weekly.

profiles.description: Confer with management to develop or implement personnel policies or procedures.
profiles.rationale: To 'confer with management to develop or implement personnel policies or procedures' carries organizational and legal accountability requiring human judgment; SHRM policy development guidance indicates ~1 hour weekly on ongoing policy work.

profiles.description: Evaluate recruitment or selection criteria to ensure conformance to professional, statistical, or testing standards, recommending revisions, as needed.
profiles.rationale: Evaluating 'recruitment or selection criteria to ensure conformance to ... standards' can be analyzed by AI but final validation and recommendations need a human; SHRM/EEOC selection validation guidance suggests ~60 minutes per active review.

profiles.description: Contact job applicants to inform them of the status of their applications.
profiles.rationale: 'Contact job applicants to inform them of the status of their applications' is standardized messaging that can be automated via ATS; ATS vendor benchmarks show ~2–5 minutes manually per applicant reduced to ~1 minute of review.

profiles.description: Conduct exit interviews and ensure that necessary employment termination paperwork is completed.
profiles.rationale: 'Conduct exit interviews' and ensure 'termination paperwork' involves sensitive in-person conversation and risk, so a human must lead; SHRM offboarding guidance estimates ~30–60 minutes per exit.

profiles.description: Interview job applicants to obtain information on work history, training, education, or job skills.
profiles.rationale: 'Interview job applicants' can be supported by AI (question guides, transcription, summaries) but requires a human to run and assess the interview; LinkedIn Talent Solutions benchmarks typical phone screens at ~30 minutes.

profiles.description: Perform searches for qualified job candidates, using sources such as computer databases, networking, Internet recruiting resources, media advertisements, job fairs, recruiting firms, or employee referrals.
profiles.rationale: Performing 'searches for qualified job candidates' can be AI-augmented (sourcing, boolean, outreach drafts) but humans curate and engage, so assist; LinkedIn Talent Solutions reports ~1–2 hours of sourcing per open requisition.

profiles.description: Provide management with information or training related to interviewing, performance appraisals, counseling techniques, or documentation of performance issues.
profiles.rationale: Providing management with 'information or training' is content AI can help prepare but live delivery and coaching require a human; SHRM training guidelines place typical sessions at ~60 minutes.

profiles.description: Analyze employment-related data and prepare required reports.
profiles.rationale: Analyzing employment-related data and preparing 'required reports' is standardized reporting that BI/HRIS can automate end-to-end; ATS/HRIS benchmarks show ~30–60 minutes weekly manually reduced to ~1–2 minutes to review.

profiles.description: Advise management on organizing, preparing, or implementing recruiting or retention programs.
profiles.rationale: Advising management on 'organizing, preparing, or implementing recruiting or retention programs' can be drafted by AI from data but requires human consultation and buy-in, so assist; SHRM program design benchmarks suggest ~1 hour per active initiative.

profiles.description: Develop or implement recruiting strategies to meet current or anticipated staffing needs.
profiles.rationale: To 'develop or implement recruiting strategies' AI can generate market-informed plans but humans tailor and approve them, so assist; LinkedIn Talent Solutions market insights indicate ~45 minutes per requisition for strategy planning.

profiles.description: Coordinate with outside staffing agencies to secure temporary employees, based on departmental needs.
profiles.rationale: 'Coordinate with outside staffing agencies' can be templated by AI (briefs, emails) but negotiation and oversight are human, so assist; SHRM vendor management benchmarks show ~30 minutes per open-requisition touchpoint.

profiles.description: Evaluate selection or testing techniques by conducting research or follow-up activities and conferring with management or supervisory personnel.
profiles.rationale: Evaluating 'selection or testing techniques' via 'research or follow-up' can be AI-supported with analyses but requires human decision-making and manager consultation, so assist; SHRM/EEOC testing-standards work typically takes ~60 minutes per review.
